content: Vaccines work by training your immune system to recognize and fight harmful germs without making you sick. Think of it like a practice drill for your body’s defense team. When you receive a vaccine, it introduces a safe, harmless piece of a virus or bacteria—just enough for your immune system to see and learn from. This “preview” helps your body prepare by building a memory of the threat. Then, if you ever encounter the actual germ, your immune system can respond quickly and effectively, stopping illness before it takes hold. In this way, vaccines act as a smart, proactive tool, giving your body the knowledge it needs to protect your health. By preparing your immune system ahead of time, vaccines help keep you—and those around you—safe and healthy.
